---
title: "Beyond tools"
subtitle: "Programming with LLMs for Data Practitioners"
author: "Julia Silge | Posit PBC"
footer: "<https://juliasilge.github.io/applied-stats-byu-2026/>"
highlight-style: atom-one
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    transition: fade
    preview-links: auto
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-image: "../images/undraw_artificial-intelligence_43qa.svg"
      data-background-size: "auto 55%"
      data-background-position: "82% 85%"
      data-background-repeat: "no-repeat"
---

# Beyond tools {background-image="../images/undraw_artificial-intelligence_43qa.svg" background-size="auto 55%" background-position="82% 85%" background-repeat="no-repeat"}

A look at higher-level patterns: querychat, MCP, and agents.

# querychat {background-image="../images/undraw_artificial-intelligence_43qa.svg" background-size="auto 55%" background-position="82% 85%" background-repeat="no-repeat"}
## Chatting with your data (10 min)

-   A drop-in Shiny module that turns a data frame into a chat-driven explorer
-   The model writes the SQL or dplyr; the app runs it

## Your turn

::: {.callout-note icon=false}

## Activity

Open `17-querychat` and add querychat to an existing Shiny app.
:::

{{< countdown minutes=10 >}}

# MCP {background-image="../images/undraw_artificial-intelligence_43qa.svg" background-size="auto 55%" background-position="82% 85%" background-repeat="no-repeat"}
## Model Context Protocol (10 min)

-   A common standard for exposing tools and resources to LLM clients
-   Why this matters: write a server once, use it from many clients
-   MCP support is growing in Positron, VS Code, Claude Desktop, etc.

## Your turn

::: {.callout-note icon=false}

## Activity

Open `18-mcp` and connect an MCP server to ellmer or chatlas:

-   Options to try: ArXiV, fetcher, git-ingest, github, context7.
-   Notice: the model now has access to tools you did not write.
:::

{{< countdown minutes=15 >}}

# Agents {background-image="../images/undraw_artificial-intelligence_43qa.svg" background-size="auto 55%" background-position="82% 85%" background-repeat="no-repeat"}
## What is an agent? (30 min)

-   Hadley / Simon Willison short definition: an LLM with a read tool and a write tool
-   The "you know it when you see it" definition: autonomous, long-context, minimal intervention
-   Demo: a working agent (databot-style) doing a real task end to end

# The future of AI {background-image="../images/undraw_artificial-intelligence_43qa.svg" background-size="auto 55%" background-position="82% 85%" background-repeat="no-repeat"}
## Hopes and fears (30 min)

-   Where these tools seem to be going
-   What to watch out for: cost, lock-in, evaluation, trust
-   Open discussion: what does this change about your day-to-day work?

# Wrap-up {background-image="../images/undraw_artificial-intelligence_43qa.svg" background-size="auto 55%" background-position="82% 85%" background-repeat="no-repeat"}

-   Feedback survey
-   What we will pick up on Day 2 (Build *with* AI)
-   Thanks!


# Thanks!

<center>

<img src="https://github.com/juliasilge.png" style="border-radius: 50%;" width="300px"/>

[{{< fa brands github >}} \@juliasilge](https://github.com/juliasilge)

[{{< fa brands bluesky >}} \@juliasilge.com](https://bsky.app/profile/juliasilge.com)

[{{< fa link >}} juliasilge.com](https://juliasilge.com/)

</center>

::: footer
Illustrations from [unDraw](https://undraw.co/)
:::
